name: e11 - Permit and Document Expiry Tracking

nodes.name: Block PO; require valid ministry permit
nodes.documentation: PO issue is blocked for the regulated item until a valid ministry permit (within date AND with sufficient remaining quantity) is attached. Buyer must obtain/renew the permit and attach it. [SCOR OE4 | ISO 8.4.3 | source: AB hard permit requirement]

nodes.name: Attach ministry permit to PO; reserve quantity
nodes.documentation: Buyer attaches the valid ministry permit to the PO. System reserves orderQuantity against permit.remainingQuantity (remainingQuantity -= orderQuantity on PO issue) so the permit is consumed by quantity as orders draw it down. Captures permitNumber, issuingMinistry, expiryDate, totalQuantity, remainingQuantity. [SCOR OE4 | ISO 8.4.3 | source: AB permit attach + quantity consumption]

nodes.name: Suspend supplier (expired cert)
nodes.documentation: Tax/Compliance (or Quality for a quality cert) suspends the supplier: status ONBOARDED -> SUSPENDED. SUSPENDED blocks NEW POs without offboarding; existing commitments continue. permission suppliers.suspend (compliance/quality conditional). On cert renewal the supplier returns SUSPENDED -> ONBOARDED. [SCOR S1.6 | ISO 8.4.1 | source: data-model SUSPENDED state + AB cert-to-suspension]

nodes.name: Surface permit / cert status on selection
nodes.documentation: System computes and displays each attached permit/cert: status {valid | expiring-soon | expired | quantity-exhausted}, remainingQuantity, expiryDate, daysToExpiry. Emits an alert ON SELECTION (the first of two alert triggers). [SCOR OE4 | ISO 7.5 | source: AB surface-on-selection alert]

nodes.name: Audit + notify (handler + head)
nodes.documentation: System writes audit entries (category supplier/admin) for permit attach, quantity reservation, alerts, and any suspension; notifies handler + head; emits SSE. Document storage retains permits/certs with expiry + quantity per ISO 7.5 records. [SCOR OE4 | ISO 7.5 | source: platform-services audit + notifications + document storage]

nodes.name: Regulated chemical (solvent / pesticide)?
nodes.documentation: Expression: item.regulatoryClass in {solvent, pesticide, controlled-chemical}. True -> a ministry permit MUST be attached to the PO before it can issue (hard requirement). False -> standard documents only (cert/COA recommended, not a PO-blocking ministry permit). [SCOR OE4 | ISO 8.4.3 | source: AB regulated-chemical ministry permit]

nodes.name: Permit valid by date AND quantity?
nodes.documentation: Expression: today <= permit.expiryDate AND permit.remainingQuantity >= orderQuantity. A permit is consumed by QUANTITY OR DATE, whichever comes first: either condition failing makes it unusable. True -> permit usable, attach to PO. False -> permit expired or exhausted; block until a renewed/new permit is attached. [SCOR OE4 | ISO 8.4.3 | source: AB permit consumed by qty OR date, whichever first]

nodes.name: Supplier cert expired?
nodes.documentation: Expression: today > supplierCert.expiryDate (an ISO/quality/trade cert on the supplier has lapsed). True -> the cert expiry FEEDS supplier suspension. False -> supplier remains usable; continue monitoring. Cert expiry is one of the suspension triggers (failed audit / expired cert / sanctions / active CAPA). [SCOR S1.6 | ISO 8.4.1 | source: AB cert expiry feeds suspension]

nodes.name: Item selected on requisition / PO
nodes.documentation: Trigger: a buyer selects an item on a requisition or PO. Documents attach at two levels: permanent against the supplier (MSDS, vendor registration, COA, certs) and per-item permits. Permits and certs carry BOTH an expiry date AND a quantity allowance. On selection, the system surfaces the item's permit/cert status inline. [SCOR OE4 | ISO 7.5 | source: AB document expiry + quantity, surface on selection]

nodes.name: Compliant item proceeds; alerts armed
nodes.documentation: Item proceeds with a valid permit attached (where required), quantity reserved, expiry alerts armed (selection + 1-week), and supplier compliance enforced (suspension on lapsed cert). [SCOR OE4 | source: AB]

nodes.name: Expiry alert >= 1 week before
nodes.documentation: Timer: at >= 1 week (7 days) before permit/cert expiryDate, fire the SECOND alert trigger to the handler (buyer) + head. Also fires if remainingQuantity falls below a configurable low threshold. Two alert triggers total: on item selection AND >= 1 week before expiry. [SCOR OE4 | ISO 7.5 | source: AB alert on selection + >=1 week before expiry, to handler + head]
